Details: MTBE removal efficiency was dependent on availability of additional source of hydroxyl radicals. In one study, pulsed-UV irradiation, which emits UV light below 200nm, was able to degrade MTBE (40 to 75%). In another study involving indirect photolysis, caused by UV irradiation in presence of titanium dioxide, up to 80% MTBE was reduced. MTBE removal efficiency increased with decreasing initial concentrations of MTBE, and increasing doses of TiO2 (0.5 to 3 g/L).
